sections.paragraph:
(by aphæresis, apparently for נְהֹוד, from the root ذهد to lift oneself up, to become lofty, Med. Damma to be eminent, beautiful [“swelling”].

(1) majesty.-(a) used of the majesty of God; often joined with הָדָר Psa 21:6 96:6 104:1 111:3 Job 40:10.-(b) of princes and kings, 1Ch 29:25 Dan 11:21 compare Num 27:20.-(c) of a voice, Isa 30:30 Job 39:20.

(2) splendour, freshness, beauty; Dan 10:8, הֹודִי נֶהְפַּךְ עָלַי “my freshness (i.e. the lively colour of my face) was changed in me,” ich verfärbte mich (vor Schrecken); Hos 14:6, כַּזַּיִת הֹודֹו “his freshness like an olive tree.” Used of ornaments, Zec 10:3, 6:13 .

(3) [Hod], pr.n. 1Ch 7:37.
